vision_report.compositional_truth_check: Registry vs. Reality: P1 (Yellow Pot) is present but appears to be empty soil/dormant. P2/P4 (Black Pot) contains a single, struggling Pothos-like cutting. P3 (Black Pot) is present with the rabbit anchor. The registry is largely inaccurate regarding current biomass.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil, no signs of String of Nickels.
plant_audit.P2_P4_Combined: Single, chlorotic, thin-stemmed cutting remaining in the black pot. Appears to be a Pothos propagation.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos cutting remains stable but shows no new growth.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and moist across all pots.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: Presence of crushed eggshells in the P3/P4 pot as a soil amendment/intervention. A small electronic component (IC chip/header) has appeared on the desk surface in later images.

temporal_deltas.T_earliest_to_T_5: Initial state shows a single leaf cutting in the black pot.
temporal_deltas.T_4_to_T_3: Introduction of a white paper/receipt and an electronic component on the desk surface.
temporal_deltas.T_2_to_Current: The plant in the black pot shows slight postural drooping; the desk environment remains static.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of severe decline. The lack of foliage in P1 and the absence of the registered species in P2/P4 suggest a failure of the initial planting. The remaining Pothos cutting is the only viable specimen, though it shows signs of stress.
anomalies: Electronic component (IC header) on desk surface
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical environment is currently under-populated and struggling. The registry does not match the visual evidence, indicating a high rate of mortality for the original specimens. The presence of non-botanical debris (electronics, paper) suggests the desk is being used as a workspace, which may be impacting the plants' micro-climate. The remaining Pothos cutting is the only active biological element.
